content:
Canonical URL: Republished from munonye.com. Full code on GitHub.

рҹ”— The missing link вҖ” You learned @Tool agents in our Spring AI agents tutorial and MCP basics in M9-A. This Spring AI MCP bridge tutorial connects them: your ChatClient calls external MCP tool servers the same way Cursor does.

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is how IDE agents discover tools. Spring AI is how you ship AI in production APIs. The Spring AI MCP bridge lets one tool server serve both вҖ” no duplicate integrations.

This hands-on guide is part of the AI Developer Tutorials hub. Time: ~45 minutes. Prerequisites: Spring AI first REST endpoint (M7-A), OpenAI API key, Node.js (for the sample MCP server).

Table of contents

вҖў Why bridge MCP and Spring AI?

вҖў Architecture

вҖў Step 1 вҖ” Dependencies

вҖў Step 2 вҖ” Configure MCP client

вҖў Step 3 вҖ” ChatClient + ToolCallbackProvider

вҖў Step 4 вҖ” REST endpoint

вҖў Step 5 вҖ” Test

вҖў Bonus вҖ” Expose Java tools as MCP server

вҖў Production checklist

Why bridge MCP and Spring AI? {#why-bridge}

Approach
Where tools run
Best for

@Tool (M13, M14)
Inside Spring Boot JVM
CRUD agents tied to your services

MCP server (M9-A)
Separate process / remote host
Shared tools for Cursor + APIs

Spring AI MCP bridge
Spring Boot orchestrates MCP clients
Production agents using ecosystem tools

рҹ’Ў Real-world pattern: Your team builds an MCP server for internal docs search. Developers use it in Cursor. With this bridge, the same server powers your customer-facing Spring Boot agent вҖ” one tool implementation, two consumers.

Related reading: Add AI to Angular CRUD app (M14) for in-process @Tool on an existing app В· Function calling from Angular (M8-B) for structured tool UX.

Architecture {#architecture}
